title: RoleMailboxThread + Message typed primitives (Rust + Postgres schema)
goal: Land the foundational durable RoleMailboxThread and RoleMailboxMessage Rust types and Postgres migration for the cluster X mailbox preservation slice. Implements the thread lifecycle state machine (open / awaiting_response / waiting_on_linked_authority / escalated / resolved / expired / archived) and the message delivery state machine (queued / delivered / acknowledged / replied / ignored / failed / dead_lettered) per spec v02.186 §02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsection [ADD v02.173]. This MT extends the KERNEL-002 role_mailbox surfaces (which seeded the export folder) with the typed thread + message authority types required by every downstream X.1 MT.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/mod.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/thread.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/message.rs + src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/lifecycle.rs; new Postgres migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/NNN_role_mailbox_threads_messages.sql; unit tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_types_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: KERNEL-002 role_mailbox export folder already exists in main (.GOV/roles_shared/exports/role_mailbox/); this MT layers the typed Rust + Postgres primitives on top. No KERNEL-004 prerequisite MTs.
implementation_notes: Per spec v02.186 §02-system-architecture.md role mailbox subsections [ADD v02.173] and [ADD v02.176]: define `RoleMailboxThread { thread_id: Uuid (v7 per HBR-INT-008), title: String, linked_record_kind: LinkedRecordKind (WP|MT|RGF|FREEFORM), linked_record_id: Option<String>, lifecycle_state: ThreadLifecycleState, executor_kind_allowlist: Vec<ExecutorKind>, claim_mode: ClaimMode (Exclusive|Handoff|Open), lease_duration_secs: Option<u32>, takeover_policy: TakeoverPolicy, response_authority_scope: ResponseAuthorityScope, created_at_utc, updated_at_utc, expires_at_utc: Option, archived_at_utc: Option }`. Define `RoleMailboxMessage { message_id: Uuid v7, thread_id: Uuid v7, message_type: MessageType (placeholder enum here — populated by MT-179), from_role: RoleId, to_roles: Vec<RoleId>, expected_response: Option<ExpectedResponse>, expires_at_utc: Option, delivery_state: MessageDeliveryState, body: serde_json::Value, parent_message_id: Option<Uuid v7>, created_at_utc }`. ThreadLifecycleState transitions enforced by `lifecycle::transition(current, requested) -> Result<ThreadLifecycleState, InvalidTransition>` — must reject illegal transitions (e.g., `archived -> open`); allowed transitions follow spec lifecycle subsection. Same pattern for MessageDeliveryState. Postgres migration creates tables `role_mailbox_thread` and `role_mailbox_message` with FKs and indexes on (lifecycle_state, expires_at_utc) and (thread_id, created_at_utc). All Uuid mint sites MUST use uuid::Uuid::now_v7() per HBR-INT-008. Add serde + sqlx (postgres) + uuid (with v7 feature) + thiserror to Cargo.toml. Tests: (a) round-trip serde for Thread + Message; (b) lifecycle state machine exhaustive transition matrix; (c) Postgres migration runs cleanly against a fresh template (use sqlx::migrate! and a test container — see KERNEL-003 conftest pattern).
risk_if_missed: Without typed thread + message primitives, every X.1 MT reinvents the lifecycle state machine and the Postgres schema, causing silent semantic drift between mailbox tools and validators. The folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-v1 stub intent (LLM role-to-role coordination without operator copy-paste, chat-is-not-state preservation) cannot land without this foundation.
validator_focus: Lifecycle and delivery state machines reject every illegal transition with a typed error; Uuid v7 used at every mint site (no Uuid::new_v4 anywhere); Postgres migration is replay-safe (re-running on an already-migrated db is a no-op or rejects cleanly per spec §2.3.13.4.1).
acceptance_criteria_refs: PRESERVED_INTENT:WP-1-Role-Mailbox-v1 (thread/message types with deterministic repo export)

minimum_controls: Lifecycle state machine has an exhaustive transition matrix test covering every (from, to) pair including rejections.
Postgres migration is idempotent and reversible per spec §2.3.13.4 migration framework.
Uuid v7 enforced via a compile-time wrapper or a runtime assertion in mint helpers per HBR-INT-008.
